title: New U-Boot flaws could enable stealthy firmware attacks
description: Six vulnerabilities in the widely used U-Boot bootloader have been discovered that could allow attackers to execute malicious code during device boot, potentially enabling stealthy firmware attacks that compromise security protections and install persistent malware. [...]
author: Lawrence Abrams

title: Ryuk ransomware member pleads guilty in the US, faces 15 years in prison
description: A 34-year-old Armenian man has pleaded guilty to hacking U.S. companies and deploying the infamous Ryuk ransomware to encrypt their systems. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: Police suspects Dutch hackers were involved in Odido breach
description: The Dutch National Police (Politie) says it has found "strong indications" that Dutch hackers have been involved in a February breach at the telecommunications provider Odido. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: Progress urges ShareFile admins to shut down servers over “credible” threat
description: Progress Software is emailing ShareFile customers who use Storage Zone Controllers to immediately shut down their servers after identifying what it describes as a "credible external security threat" targeting the on-premises secure file-sharing software. [...]
author: Lawrence Abrams

title: Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
description: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the official Docker image for the Gitea self-hosted Git service that allows attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators. [...]
author: Bill Toulas

title: Money launderer accused of stealing seized crypto while in prison
description: A Bulgarian national has been charged with stealing $290,000 in government-seized cryptocurrency while serving 121 months in prison for helping launder millions stolen from American fraud victims. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan

title: The Replicant in Your Directory: AI Agents and the Identity Security Gap
description: AI agents are accelerating the growth of non-human identities, making it harder for organizations to understand what exists, who owns it, and what it can access. Netwrix explains why stronger visibility and identity governance are essential as AI expands the enterprise attack surface. [...]
author: Sponsored by Netwrix

title: Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
description: The Zimbra security team urged customers to patch a critical vulnerability affecting the Classic Web Client used to access the Zimbra Collaboration suite. [...]
author: Sergiu Gatlan
